items.original_candidate: (uncertain — possibly Marvin Rich (CORE), Marvin Surkin (IPS), or Marvin Hill)
items.evidence: The Whisper rendering is correct. Henderson's transcript places this name in DC immediately after Resurrection City (June 1968), when she became a student at the Institute for Policy Studies (IPS) and names 'Frank Smith, Marvin Holloway, Ivanhoe Donaldson' as fellows there. Marvin Holloway is independently and authoritatively documented as an Africanist scholar and fellow at the Institute for Policy Studies during this exact era, who articulated Pan-Africanist principles alongside Courtland Cox, was an early member of Afro-American Resources, Inc. (the organization behind Drum and Spear Bookstore, where Henderson worked immediately after IPS, per the very next lines of the transcript), and shepherded the Palestinian-poetry collection 'Enemy of the Sun' through Drum and Spear Press. Both Frank Smith and Ivanhoe Donaldson were also SNCC veterans at IPS, exactly matching the cohort Henderson names. The original 'uncertain' candidates (Marvin Rich/CORE; Marvin Surkin; Marvin Hill) are not corroborated as IPS fellows or as part of the Smith/Donaldson SNCC-to-IPS-to-Drum-and-Spear DC circle.
